pr.title: Match gmadventure, link, and ninja apply
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 2224 matched the gmadventure object by extracting a repeated gm_8016A22C setup sequence into a shared local helper, marking melee/gm/gmadventure.c as Matching in configure.py, and renaming several local .sdata2 symbols from gm_804DACxx address-style names to local @ labels. The bot report recorded one new match for main/melee/gm/gmadventure: gm_801B4974 reaching 100.00%, with code/link/data progress.

key_files.role: Source match cleanup: introduced static helper gm_8016A22C_inline and replaced duplicated gm_8016A22C argument-building blocks in gm_801B4974 and inline0.
Build configuration: changed melee/gm/gmadventure.c from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk around MatchingFor object list

key_files.role: Symbol metadata: renamed six local .sdata2 entries at 0x804DAC18-0x804DAC2C from gm_804DACxx names to local @ labels.

what_changed.text: Added static void gm_8016A22C_inline(MinorScene* arg0, UnkAdventureData* temp_r29), which computes count via gm_8017E48C, looks up gm_803DE650_t data via gm_8017E4C4(arg0->idx), calls gm_8016A22C with the repeated argument list, and sets temp_r29->x0.xC.x11 = 1.
Replaced the inline gm_8016A22C setup block in gm_801B4974 with gm_8016A22C_inline(arg0, temp_r29), removing local variables count and temp_r25_2 from that function.
Replaced the equivalent setup block in inline0 with gm_8016A22C_inline(scene, adv_data), while preserving the existing unused calls to gm_80169520 and gm_80169540 before the helper call.
Changed local .sdata2 symbols gm_804DAC18, gm_804DAC1C, gm_804DAC20, gm_804DAC24, gm_804DAC28, and gm_804DAC2C to local labels @217, @218, @244, @306, @307, and @330 respectively.
Bot report recorded matched code +436 bytes, linked code +5092 bytes, linked data +1996 bytes, and one new match: main/melee/gm/gmadventure gm_801B4974 from 99.04% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: A repeated long call setup was factored into one file-local helper rather than duplicated in two call sites, reducing source duplication while preserving the side effect that marks x0.xC.x11 as initialized.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/gm/gmadventure.c helper addition and two replacement call sites

smart_moves.text: The PR paired the source match with the narrow build configuration flip from NonMatching to Matching for the same object.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows one new match for main/melee/gm/gmadventure

smart_moves.text: Local .sdata2 labels were normalized away from address-style gm_804DACxx names to local @ labels, consistent with the diff evidence that these entries were local-scope data symbols.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When the same long call argument construction appears in multiple nearby paths, a small file-local helper can be a viable match shape if the helper captures the shared computations and required side effects exactly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: duplicated gm_8016A22C setup replaced by gm_8016A22C_inline in two locations
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows gm_801B4974 reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For local-scope data entries, address-style global-looking labels may be replaced by local @ labels when the symbol metadata and matching work identify them as local literals/data rather than semantic globals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_804DAC18-gm_804DAC2C renamed to @217-@330 with scope:local metadata

naming_conventions.name: gm_8016A22C_inline
naming_conventions.convention: The helper name keeps the callee address-style name and appends _inline, indicating it encapsulates an inlined call/setup pattern rather than claiming recovered semantic meaning.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static void gm_8016A22C_inline(MinorScene* arg0, UnkAdventureData* temp_r29)

naming_conventions.convention: Local .sdata2 entries were represented as @ local labels rather than gm_804DACxx address-style symbols.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Extract duplicated source sequence into a static helper used by both the direct scene path and the inline0 path.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/gm/gmadventure.c gm_8016A22C_inline addition and call-site replacements
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The bot report supports that this contributed to a match for gm_801B4974, but the PR does not include objdiff logs beyond the automated progress report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Flip configure.py object status only after source and symbol metadata changes for gmadventure.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev[bot] report with 1 new match
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: No separate build command or manual verification transcript is present in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust local .sdata2 symbol labels to match expected local label ownership/order.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The slice shows the metadata edit but not the underlying linker/map rationale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Factor repeated long call setup into a local helper when it preserves exact computations and side effects
accepted_candidate_records.text: In gmadventure, two duplicated gm_8016A22C setup blocks were replaced by a static helper gm_8016A22C_inline that computes count, loads scene data, calls gm_8016A22C, and sets x0.xC.x11. The bot report recorded gm_801B4974 reaching 100.00%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use local @ labels for local-scope .sdata2 entries when semantic/global ownership is not shown
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR changed six local .sdata2 entries at 0x804DAC18-0x804DAC2C from gm_804DACxx address-style names to local @ labels while keeping their local scope metadata.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a general rule that helper extraction always improves matching; this slice only supports the specific repeated gm_8016A22C setup case and the bot-reported gm_801B4974 match.
Do not promote the symbol renames as proof of data ownership rationale beyond local-scope relabeling; the slice lacks map/linker explanation.
Do not claim human reviewer approval or intent; no review comments or reviews are present.
Do not claim ninja apply behavior from the title alone; no ninja command output is included.
